```{r}
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(brms)
library(tidybayes)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
df <- read.csv(here("data", "cleaned", "nyts_2021_2023_clean.csv"))
head(df)
```

```{r}
df$ecig_current <- as.integer(df$ecig_current)

df$sex   <- factor(df$sex)
df$grade <- factor(df$grade)
df$year  <- factor(df$year)

# for scaling purposes
df$age         <- scale(df$age)
df$sm_usage    <- scale(df$sm_usage)
df$sm_freq     <- scale(df$sm_freq)
df$post_freq   <- scale(df$post_freq)
df$interact_freq <- scale(df$interact_freq)

head(df)
```

```{r}

model <- brm(
  ecig_current ~ age + sex + grade +
    sm_usage + sm_freq + post_freq + interact_freq +
    platform_tt + platform_insta + platform_yt +
    creator_influencers + creator_brands_sellers + creator_public_health,
  
  data = df,
  family = bernoulli(link = "logit"),
  
  prior = c(
    set_prior("normal(0, 1)", class = "b"),
    set_prior("normal(0, 2)", class = "Intercept")
  ),
  
  chains = 4,
  iter = 2000,
  seed = 123
)

```

## Saving Results

```{r}
# Posterior summary of all coefficients
fixed_effects <- as.data.frame(fixef(model))
fixed_effects$predictor <- rownames(fixed_effects)

# write.csv(fixed_effects, here("model", "fixed_effects.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```

## Saving Results

```{r}
# Marginal predictions across a key variable, e.g. platform
newdata <- expand.grid(
  platform_tt = c(0, 1),
  platform_insta = c(0, 1),
  platform_yt = c(0, 1),
  # hold others at reference/mean
  age = 0, sex = "1", grade = "9",
  sm_usage = 0, sm_freq = 0, post_freq = 0, interact_freq = 0,
  creator_influencers = 0, creator_brands_sellers = 0, creator_public_health = 0
)

preds <- fitted(model, newdata = newdata)
newdata$prob <- preds[, "Estimate"]
newdata$lower <- preds[, "Q2.5"]
newdata$upper <- preds[, "Q97.5"]

# write.csv(newdata, here("model", "predicted_probs.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```
